title: jigg state v0.2
description: Standalone sidecar file recording one user's progress on a puzzle. Not embedded in the .jigg archive.

Pos.description: Normalized position, image-relative. Stage 0 (freeform): [0,1] = within image bounds, clamped [-2,3]. Stage 1+ (managed): tray-relative hint, or absent if UI auto-sorts.

puzzleId.description: UUID v4. Must match the id field in the target .jigg manifest. Readers MUST reject mismatches.

manifestHash.description: SHA-256 of manifest.json at save time. Used for three-tier integrity check on load.

lastPlayed.description: ISO 8601 datetime of the most recent save.

gridDimensions.description: Required when puzzle has no pieces.json (procedural grid). If present and does not match app-derived grid, app MUST start a new session.

stages.description: Named environments. stages[0] MUST have id 0 and type 'freeform' (the workspace). Additional stages are user-defined trays.

id.description: 0 = workspace. 1+ = user-defined tray. Referenced by pieces[].stageId.

type.description: freeform: pos/rot/z are meaningful, snap and physics run. managed: engine renders a sorted list, pos may be absent or tray-relative.

pieces.description: Current state of all tracked pieces. Pieces absent from this array are treated as not yet placed.

id.description: Piece ID from pieces.json (whimsy) or grid_{col}_{row} (raw procedural).

rot.description: Degrees [0, 360). Persisted across all stages. Managed stages MAY ignore this for UI layout but MUST preserve it for state integrity.

z.description: Layering order. Higher = on top. Meaningful for freeform stages. Managed stages SHOULD respect this if the tray allows overlapping pieces.

c.description: Cluster ID. Pieces sharing the same c value move as a rigid body. Absent = piece is unconnected.

stageId.description: ID of the stage this piece is on. Absent or null = stage 0 (workspace). Writers SHOULD omit for workspace pieces.
